text.title: Affiliate Recruitment and Enablement
text.summary: Affiliate type selection, outreach approach, asset library requirements, and onboarding sequence design.

rule.statement: Prioritize niche newsletter writers as first-recruit targets; they typically offer the highest CPM-equivalent ROI versus broad bloggers because their audience trust is higher and topic alignment is tighter.
rule.rationale: Newsletter writers have direct inbox access to a pre-qualified audience. Open rates above 25% with prior sponsored content signal monetization willingness and audience trust.

section.title: Type Reference
section.p: High-value types in order of typical effort-to-revenue: newsletter writers (low effort, targeted), course creators (low effort, curriculum integration), power users/existing customers (low effort, authentic voice), comparison sites (medium effort, high intent traffic), content bloggers (medium effort, SEO value), agencies recommending to clients (medium effort, B2B).

section.title: Outreach

rule.statement: Personalize outreach with a specific reference to the affiliate's recent content before mentioning the program; generic "partnership opportunity" emails get ignored.
rule.rationale: Affiliates receive mass outreach constantly. A specific reference proves you are a real reader and increases reply rate significantly.

rule.statement: State the commission structure and average monthly earnings range in the first outreach message; affiliates will not reply to vague "great opportunity" messages.
rule.rationale: Time is the affiliate's constraint. Concrete numbers allow instant triage. "Our top affiliates earn $X/month" backed by real distribution data also sets honest expectations.

section.reference: Welcome email with link, dashboard access, quick-start steps

rule.statement: Provide at minimum: unique tracking links, banner ads (multiple sizes), email swipe copy, social post copy, demo video, and case studies before recruiting affiliates.
rule.rationale: Affiliates promote what is easy to use. Without assets, even motivated affiliates stall. The asset library quality is the single biggest predictor of activation rate.

antipattern.description: Launching the program without an asset library and expecting affiliates to create their own promotional materials.
antipattern.reason: Affiliates abandon programs that require significant creative work; they move to programs that make promotion frictionless.

rule.statement: Run a structured onboarding sequence: Day 0 welcome + credentials, Day 1 dashboard tutorial, Day 3 best-performing strategies, Day 7 check-in, Day 14 first-sale tips, Day 30 recap.
rule.rationale: 80% of affiliates who don't produce a sale in the first 30 days never will. A structured onboarding shortens time-to-first-sale and increases the activation rate above 30%.

rule.statement: Provide a "top 10 starter playbook" covering the highest-converting tactics from existing affiliates so new partners can copy proven approaches immediately.
rule.rationale: New affiliates lack context on what messaging and channels work. Sharing winning tactics reduces experimentation time and aligns affiliate copy with brand standards.

rule.statement: Recompute LTV quarterly and adjust commission tiers accordingly; never let affiliates discover a commission cut mid-campaign.
rule.rationale: Affiliates plan promotional calendars around commission rates. Surprise cuts destroy trust and trigger public complaints in affiliate communities.
